name: catdef + roledef + orgdef ecosystem
mission: Steward the catdef substrate spec and the catdef-family consumer specs (roledef, orgdef, future xxxdef specs) as open standards. Demonstrate empirically that AI roles can be portable across runtimes, that organizations can be machine-readable, and that the catdef substrate scales across multiple consumer specs.
scope: catdef substrate + roledef + orgdef + future catdef-family consumer specs (memodef, openmemo, openorg, aigp). Out of scope: AWS Thinkbox OpenJD, Oracle Open Agent Specification, chrisbarry/openagent — adjacent specs in different technical domains. Also out of scope: vendor-specific runtime tooling, single-vendor accommodations, closed-source forks.
governance_model: Bounded AI authority + human-maintainer ratification. AI sessions draft, validate, decide, sign off; human maintainers (currently a single human steward) hold final merge authority on every commit. Vendors do not own the spec — Anthropic, xAI, OpenAI, Google, Meta, and any future runtime are equal citizens. Pattern-promotion (recognizing emergent patterns in submissions and promoting them to spec text) is a strategist responsibility; spec-text drafting is a maintainer responsibility; canonical implementation work is a canonical-implementor responsibility; library validation is a validator responsibility — explicit role-separation is load-bearing for the bounded-authority discipline.

positions.name: Human Steward
positions.description: The single human at the apex. Final sign-off authority on every merge, version bump, governance decision, deploy, and DNS change. Authorizes AI-session work but does not author it. Routes inter-session memos by handing prompts (current state) or notifying receivers of new memos (future state).

positions.name: Senior Open Standards Strategist (abstract)
positions.description: Abstract parent role. Not directly staffed — sessions instantiate via one of the derived strategist roles (catdef, roledef, orgdef). Included here for lineage transparency: catdef-strategist v2.0.0 and roledef-strategist v1.0.0 declare metadata.derived_from pointing at this abstract.

positions.name: catdef Strategist

incumbent.session_arc: Bootstrapping session 2026-04-25 to 2026-04-26 — same arc that bootstrapped catdef v1.4, the roledef library v0.1, the strategist-formalization sequence (PRs #18-#20 in roledef), and the orgdef bootstrap (PR #1 in orgdef which is THIS artifact).

positions.description: Stewards the catdef substrate spec and coordinates catdef-family ecosystem decisions. Triages catdef proposals using catdef-spec/CLAUDE.md heuristics (first-matching-branch discipline). Enforces value-that-doesn't-move governance with human-ratification escalation. Drafts CA-NNN decision artifacts.

positions.name: roledef Strategist

incumbent.session_arc: Same arc as catdef-strategist (one session played both roles during the bootstrap). Role-separation acknowledged in decision artifacts; expected to split as the ecosystem matures and runtimes/sessions specialize.

positions.description: Stewards the roledef consumer spec. Triages roledef library submissions against schema + additive-only invariant for derivations + dual-review pattern for production-extracted submissions. Maintains the runtime-conformance methodology (4-runtime classification per RUNTIME_AMENABILITY.md). Stewards the abstract→derived showcase pattern.

positions.name: orgdef Strategist

incumbent.session_arc: Currently played informally by the catdef-strategist session arc (bootstrapping). roledef forthcoming as a derivation of senior-open-standards-strategist; will join the canonical roledef library when orgdef bootstrap stabilizes.
incumbent.note: No roledef yet; eventual derivation from senior-open-standards-strategist is a forward work item.

positions.description: Stewards the orgdef consumer spec. Triages orgdef library submissions against schema + roledef-reference resolution + connectedness checks. THIS artifact is its first piece of stewardship work.

positions.name: catdef Maintainer

incumbent.session_arc: Currently no dedicated maintainer session has been spun up; catdef-strategist has been doing maintainer work in delegated capacity during the v1.4 release.
incumbent.note: Per catdef-spec/CLAUDE.md the maintainer role is defined; the role's incumbent has not been formally roledef-extracted. Future work item.

positions.description: Drafts catdef spec text (CATDEF_SPEC.md, CATIO_SPEC.md, MCP_REFERENCE.md). Processes feedback. Runs editorial passes. Operates per catdef-spec/CLAUDE.md operating manual. Bounded authority — drafts, doesn't merge.

positions.name: roledef Maintainer

incumbent.session_arc: Currently no dedicated maintainer; roledef-strategist has done maintainer work in delegated capacity (drafting README, CONTRIBUTING, CLAUDE, WRAPPER_PROMPT, RUNTIME_AMENABILITY, SCHEMA edits).

positions.description: Drafts roledef spec text. Processes feedback. Runs editorial passes. Operates per roledef CLAUDE.md.

positions.name: orgdef Maintainer

incumbent.session_arc: Currently no dedicated maintainer; orgdef-strategist has done maintainer work in delegated capacity (drafting orgdef SCHEMA, CONTRIBUTING, CLAUDE, README during the v0.1 bootstrap).

positions.description: Drafts orgdef spec text. Processes feedback. Runs editorial passes.

positions.name: roledef Validator

incumbent.session_arc: No dedicated validator session running; roledef-strategist self-validates in validator capacity per the validator-vs-strategist role-overlap acknowledged in decision artifacts. Validator-as-CI-step automation is a Known Work Item that would close this overlap.

positions.description: Validates submitted roledefs against SCHEMA.md. Produces structured PASS / PASS WITH NOTES / FAIL reports. Schema-conformance only — library-fit calls are strategist scope.

positions.name: roledef Contributor

incumbent.session_arc: Multiple sessions act as contributor across the bootstrap. Notable instances: brother-blackhat-tester (self-extracted blackhat-tester roledef + sncro-blackhat-tester derivation); brother-DangerStorm (source-project peer review + extracted senior-jaded-vc-associate). Each operates in a session-arc associated with its source-project workspace.

positions.description: Authors roledef submissions. Self-extraction by the role's incumbent is the strongest source-material pattern. Production-extraction (behavioral capture from a live AI product) requires dual-review per the established policy.

positions.name: catdef Canonical Implementor

incumbent.session_arc: Brother-canonical-implementor session arc — has shipped catdef.org PRs #1 (URL-loadable renderer + /fetch SSRF proxy), #2 (catdef-family namespaced types + tolerant flat field lookup), #3 (renderable-path linkification), and is currently working on PR #4 (template-literal escape-eating fix).
incumbent.note: Role exists informally per the catdef.org PR sequence; eventual self-extraction into a canonical roledef is a forward work item from strategist memory.

positions.description: Owns ecosystem-service code (catdef.org Worker, render.catdef.org renderer + /fetch proxy, future ecosystem services at catdef.org subdomains). Receives handoffs from strategist via prompt-and-paste (current) or memo (forthcoming via this org's x.org.memo_location). Bounded authority — drafts code, opens PRs, does not deploy or merge.

positions.name: Reference Implementor
positions.description: Maintains canonical reference data in catdef-spec/canonical/ and builds reference implementations (planned: MCP server). Defined per catdef-spec/CLAUDE.md but not currently staffed; the planned MCP server is upcoming work.

metadata.extracted_from: Self-extraction by the orgdef-strategist incumbent (currently the same session arc as catdef-strategist and roledef-strategist; role-separation acknowledged). Sources: (1) catdef-spec/CLAUDE.md role definitions for catdef-maintainer, reference-implementor, AI-maintainer; (2) roledef CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md role definitions for roledef-maintainer, roledef-validator, roledef-contributor, roledef-strategist; (3) the recently-published roledef library (8 roledefs as of 2026-04-26: roledef-contributor v1.0.0, roledef-validator v1.0.0, catdef-strategist v2.0.0, senior-jaded-vc-associate v1.0.0, blackhat-tester v1.0.0, sncro-blackhat-tester v1.0.0, senior-open-standards-strategist v1.0.0, roledef-strategist v1.0.0); (4) the catdef.org PR sequence (#1, #2, #3, in-flight #4) which surfaced the catdef-canonical-implementor role; (5) the strategist memory file for cross-cutting context; (6) the orgdef SCHEMA.md authored alongside this artifact. Provenance audit: EXTRACTED — every position and relationship in this artifact comes from documented role definitions in CLAUDE.md / CONTRIBUTING.md / roledef library / catdef.org PRs; no positions invented. FRAMING — the org's mission/scope/governance descriptions are sharpenings consistent with documented practice but not verbatim from any single source. FORMALIZATION — the relationship typing (reports_to / peer_of / derives_from / drafts_for / implements_for / validates_for / coordinates_with) is the orgdef SCHEMA.md standard relationship type set applied to documented role-relationships; this artifact is the first concrete instantiation of that schema. The x.org.memo_location declaration is the first-instance application of an emerging memo-convention pattern that was articulated by the human steward in conversation 2026-04-26 (transport-vs-content separation; memos as catdef things via x.memo.* extensions; transport mechanism agnostic — local filesystem today, MCP-as-notification future). OUT-OF-SCOPE — adjacent specs (AWS Thinkbox OpenJD, Oracle Open Agent Specification, chrisbarry/openagent) referenced for distinguishability but not part of this org; future catdef-family consumer specs (memodef, openmemo, openorg, aigp) referenced as anticipated but not yet bootstrapped.

history.change: Initial self-extraction by the orgdef-strategist incumbent. First orgdef artifact in canonical. Establishes catdef-org as the formally-described organization stewarding catdef + roledef + orgdef + future catdef-family consumer specs. Includes 12 positions (1 staffed-human, 3 staffed-ai, 8 informal/vacant), 24 relationships across 7 standard relationship types, and the first instantiation of the x.org.memo_location convention (transport-agnostic memo location declared at the org level).

x.org.memo_location.description: Filesystem path where this organization's memos live. Memos are catdef .openthing artifacts addressed via x.memo.from / x.memo.to / x.memo.subject extensions. Initially flat directory; if/when memo traffic warrants, may organize into subdirectories. The memo location is intentionally local (not in any git repo) so that memo content stays on the steward's filesystem regardless of which transport (local-filesystem-today, MCP-as-notification-future, SMTP, RSS, file-watcher) carries the notification. This separates transport from content per the design articulated 2026-04-26: transport is swappable; memo content lives where the steward controls it.

x.org.memo_extension_namespace.description: Convention for the x.memo.* extension namespace used on memo .openthing artifacts in this org's memo location. Initially informal (no formal memodef spec yet); a future memodef-spec/memodef consumer-spec is anticipated to formalize when patterns crystallize from real use. Naive agents reading this org's memos: each memo is an .openthing with the fields below in addition to standard catdef envelope fields.

x.memo.from.description: Sender's position id from this org's positions array.

x.memo.to.description: Receiver's position id, OR the literal string 'all' for org-wide announcements.

x.memo.subject.description: Short subject line for inbox scanning.
x.memo.subject.example: PR #4: fix template-literal regex escape-eating bug

x.memo.in_reply_to.description: Filename of the predecessor memo in the same /memos/ directory, when this memo is a reply or part of a thread.

x.memo.action_required.description: Whether the receiver is expected to take action, or this is informational only.

body.description: The memo body. Markdown. Use the same plain-text == Section == convention as cross-session prompts so the memo survives copy-paste flattening if a transport mechanism strips formatting.
body.example:
== Background ==

PR #3 deployed but render.catdef.org broke...

== The ask ==
...

x.org.notes.description: Operational notes about this org that don't fit cleanly into positions or relationships.
value: Single-session-plays-multiple-strategist-roles is the current bootstrap reality. Role-separation discipline is enforced via decision-artifact attribution (each decision artifact says which spec's strategist authority is being exercised) even when one session physically plays both. Future state: distinct sessions or runtimes specialize per role.

Several positions are 'informal' — operationally active but not yet roledef-extracted. Eventual self-extraction is a forward work item per strategist memory. Listed here as positions with status: informal so the org chart is accurate even when the underlying roledef artifact doesn't exist yet.

The human-steward position is the single non-AI position. Authority flow is: AI sessions draft/decide/sign off → human steward authorizes (currently by manual prompt-pasting) → human steward merges and deploys. The memo convention (this org's x.org.memo_location) is intended to reduce the human steward's transcription burden while preserving authorization role and full audit trail.
